findings.title: Immediate reset races with the still-running turn and corrupts session state
findings.description: The new `/new` fast path runs before `try_acquire_session`, so it can delete the session while another `handle_message` / `execute_in_session` invocation is still awaiting `process_turn_events` for the current turn. That older task still owns the old `event_rx`; once `kill_session()` aborts the worker, `process_turn_events()` observes the sender drop and treats it as an abnormal exit, updating DB state to `error`/`idle` and potentially sending an error mention for a turn the user explicitly reset. Because the reset path also clears `turn_participants`, `turn_initiators`, `session_skills`, and deletes the DB row first, the in-flight turn can now finish against cleared state and report the wrong participants or fallback to pinging the owner. This is a real lifecycle race between reset and the active turn, not just a cosmetic issue.
findings.suggestion: Serialize reset against the active turn instead of deleting the session immediately. For example, acquire/mark the session first, send an interrupt/reset request through the existing worker path, and only clear DB/in-memory state after the old turn has fully terminated and `process_turn_events()` has completed.

findings.title: Reset acknowledges success even when process teardown fails, orphaning the child
findings.description: The new reset path logs `kill_session()` failures and then continues clearing bot state, deleting the DB session, and sending the reset confirmation. But `kill_session()` removes the session from `SessionManager` and aborts the worker before calling `child.kill()`. If `child.kill().await` returns an error, the handle has already been removed from the map and the worker that would normally reap/clean up the child is gone. At that point the bot has reported a successful reset while the subprocess may still be alive or unreaped, and there is no remaining control path to clean it up. This creates exactly the zombie/orphan risk the reset path is supposed to avoid.
findings.suggestion: Treat any non-`NotFound` `kill_session()` error as a hard failure and do not delete state or confirm reset. Separately, make `kill_session()` teardown atomic: keep ownership until kill/wait succeeds, or explicitly `wait()`/reap on failure paths before dropping the handle.

findings.title: Skill-triggered reset has the same unsynchronized teardown bug
findings.description: The duplicated `/new` logic in `execute_in_session()` has the same behavior as the message handler path: it kills and deletes the session before coordinating with any active turn. That means resets triggered from commands/skills can also abort an in-flight turn underneath an existing `process_turn_events()` waiter, producing the same stale-state updates and false error reporting. Because the logic is duplicated, fixing only one path will leave the other buggy.
findings.suggestion: Refactor reset into a single helper that performs synchronized teardown and reuse it from both `handle_message()` and `execute_in_session()`.

summary: The diff introduces an unsafe immediate-reset path: it can tear down a session while a turn is still in flight, and it reports success even when subprocess teardown fails, which can orphan the child.
